title: Perfume: The Story of a Murderer
author: Patrick Süskind

setting.note: A Paris built from smell before sight: the reeking fish stalls of the old Innocents charnel-ground, the tanneries steaming by the Seine, and a perfumer's shop crowded onto a bridge over the river. The map anchors in the medieval heart of the city, then follows its scent-obsessed foundling south across France to the perfume town of Grasse, marked here as a deliberate excursion beyond Paris.

palette.note: A deep violet, the colour of distilled essence and of the aristocratic scent the story chases. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
